authors: Indiana University-Bloomington) is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Communication at Georgia State University
raw_title: Populist Hustlers Versus Establishment Dynasties: The Contentious Visual Framing Dual of the 2022 Kenyan Election
title_en: Populist Hustlers Versus Establishment Dynasties: The Contentious Visual Framing Dual of the 2022 Kenyan Election
title_zh: 民粹主义奋斗者与建制派王朝：2022年肯尼亚选举的争议性视觉框架对决
plain_summary_zh: 这篇研究分析了2022年肯尼亚选举期间，媒体如何通过视觉图像来塑造两种对立的政治形象：一种是代表普通民众的“民粹主义奋斗者”，另一种是代表传统政治精英的“建制派王朝”。研究探讨了这些视觉框架如何影响公众对选举的认知和争议。
plain_summary_en: This study examines how media used visual images during the 2022 Kenyan election to frame two opposing political identities: 'populist hustlers' representing ordinary citizens and 'establishment dynasties' representing traditional political elites. It explores how these visual frames shaped public perception and controversy around the election.
research_direction_zh: 该研究属于政治传播和视觉传播领域，关注数字时代媒体如何通过视觉内容构建政治叙事和影响民主进程，特别是在非洲国家的选举背景下。
research_direction_en: This research falls within political communication and visual communication studies, focusing on how media constructs political narratives through visual content in the digital age and influences democratic processes, particularly in election contexts in African countries.
